title: Numbers: Purity and Blessing
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of numbers: purity and blessing and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study numbers: purity and blessing and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What blessing did God command Aaron and his sons to pronounce over Israel in Numbers 6? Answer: The LORD bless you and keep you; the LORD make his face shine on you and be gracious to you; the LORD turn his face toward you and give you peace.
segments.correct_answer: The LORD bless you and keep you; the LORD make his face shine on you and be gracious to you; the LORD turn his face toward you and give you peace
acceptable_variants: may the lord bless you and keep you — the priestly blessing
the threefold blessing of peace and grace

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The priestly benediction in Numbers 6:24-26 commanded for Aaron and his sons to pronounce over Israel; it remains the most used benediction in Jewish and Christian worship
A voluntary vow of consecration in Numbers 6 involving abstaining from wine, not cutting hair, and avoiding corpses; Samson and John the Baptist are notable Nazirites
